witnesses.description: A finite topology on three points.
Interior and closure of {b} in the finite topology.
An open metric ball in a finite rational metric space.

checks.claim: The listed finite family of open sets is a topology.
checks.notes: The validator checks empty/universe membership and pairwise union/intersection closure.

checks.claim: The listed subset has the stated interior and closure in the finite topology.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes interior as the union of open subsets and closure by complementing interior of the complement.

checks.claim: The listed open metric ball is exactly {p0, p1}.
checks.notes: The validator checks the finite metric table and recomputes d(center, point) < radius.

checks.claim: The listed finite family is a topology even though it omits the empty set.

data.certificate: The resource regression emits DRAT, elaborates to LRAT, and checks both with check_drat/check_lrat.

checks.notes: The fixed open-set table omits the empty set, while the topology axioms require it; the source DIMACS artifact isolates that one-variable contradiction for DRAT/LRAT checking.
